title: OmniBook 7 Laptop 14" 3K OLED Intel Ultra 5 225h 32GB 512GB SSD $1198 Delivered + More @ HP eBay
body:
Deal: OmniBook 7 Laptop 14" 3K OLED Intel Ultra 5 225h 32GB 512GB SSD $1198 Delivered + More @ HP eBay

[dmski] Anyone have one of these (OmniBook) and can share thoughts on the keyboard? Looks like a toy but specs are pretty solid for price
Also, what was wrong with i3 i5 i7 i9, WTF is an Ultra chip?
[tonyjzx] 'when everything is ultra nothing is ultra'
[YasimKudomi Pape] Just grab it before it's out of stock! Don't worry too much about the CPU. The 32GB of RAM is the real selling point here.
[SoTightEcoLPI] I have work supplied one. It's a solid unit. I don't like the keyboard or ergonomics. The CPU is super strong. I'd Rather my personal X1 carbon gen 9 anyway for keyboard and ergonomics. But I am biased towards ThinkPad.
[Raj09] How's the screen? The oled is cool but puts wasted on only 14 inch
[Innately Incognito] Keyboard is probably my least favourite part of this laptop, but for the price it's good value.
If I didn't need a laptop urgently at the time, I'd probably have tried to source a Xiaomi Book Pro 14 or something.
[justtoreply] Didn't someone say one of those models had massive issues.. I'll try find the comment
Edit: no, that was the L13 Lenovo 3 in 1 AMD, touchscreen driver causes constant BSOD apparently
[tonyjzx] Memory
32 GB LPDDR5-7467 MT/s (onboard)
Hard Drive Capacity
512 GB PCIe Gen4 NVMe M.2 SSD
that's a lot of memory going down with the ship when this thing croaks
[hellolord] If this is a touch screen, would be awesome.
[justtoreply] The envy looks good but apparently it's widely known to have hinge issues.
Can anyone recommend a $1000 touchscreen that does not have hinge issues? Pretty much rules out Lenovo and HP.
[douglasac10] Physically, because it doesn't have the RAM slots so they can shave a few millimetres off and give you a thinner product.
Soldered RAM typically also runs at a higher frequency than socketed RAM, and there's a minor power saving as well.
Apparently, these benefits outweigh the fact that if the RAM is faulty you have to throw out an entire motherboard.
[congo] I think it more about "planned obsolescence"
[mafmouf] I'd have agreed with that many years ago. I can't see the ram spec trends going through the roof in 5 years or whatever this consumer grade lappy's lifetime is.
[sickfvckadam] Anyone know a decent 2 in 1 laptop to get
[surm] Can the extended warranty from HP can be bought for these?
[BennyHe2018] Teardown of Omnibook 7:
https://youtu.be/PRFzgcsZYmE?si=xLEiVigmsE60CQCs
Looks decent despite the RAM is soldered.
[leetec] lol look at all that plastic chasing @2:00, no way it will last couple years for daily carring usage. Basically a budget line laptop with aluminium wrap, which align with this price.
[Mijora1] are there any deals that include a 5060 in a 14 or 15 inch with a keyboard without the number pad? would be keen but cant find one
[Leeroy Jenkins] Not sure if you consider this a deal, but it looks great to me:
https://www.centrecom.com.au/lenovo-yoga-pro-7-15-3-2-5k-165…
[Leeroy Jenkins] This is good. Intel graphics means I can run KVM easily without all the crap with Nvidia drivers. Seems like a very good machine for daily driving Linux, but I'm concerned about the keyboard.
[FujinShu] Any good Panther Lake Core Ultra X options with an OLED screen and decent audio that runs Linux?
